description.p: Ambient Lighting or Bias Light is a technology that enhances your viewing experience by projecting colored lights onto the wall behind your TV/Monitor, matching the colors on the screen. This creates a more immersive and engaging atmosphere, making your favorite shows, movies, and games feel even more captivating.

Luciferin is a generic term for the light-emitting compound found in organisms that generate bioluminescence like Fireflies and Glow Worms. Firefly Luciferin is a Java Fast Screen Capture PC software designed for Glow Worm Luciferin firmware, those two software create the perfect Bias Lighting and Ambient Light system for PC. Differently from other solutions, Firefly and Glow Worm Luciferin are designed to work together for the best experience possible.
Best in class performance combined with ultra low CPU/GPU usage.
Advanced algorithms for smooth colors transitions and color correction. Seeing is believing.
Wireless or cabled, local or remote? Choose your flavour with MQTT support and Home Assistant integration.
Multi monitor support with single or multiple instances.
Web browser firmware installer and Web Interface.
Programmable firmware, change your microcontroller's settings on the fly.
Frequent updates, upgrade your PC software and your firmware in one click.
Automatic switching between aspect ratios based on your video content.
Made from a gamer, for gamers. No added lag, stutter free.
Multi platform, Windows and Linux ready. macOS is coming when it's ready.
If you don't want to design your own PCB and you have a soldering iron, there is Luciferin's official PCB.
Have a question? Get answered on the community.

ul.li: Update requirement: requires Glow Worm Luciferin firmware (v5.24.7)

The Luciferin Official PCB requires a "D1 Mini format" board. With the new Luciferin Module for the Official PCB, you can use a smaller board, enabling upgrades without replacing the PCB or redoing soldering or cabling. The Luciferin Ethernet Module can add Ethernet connectivity to boards that do not natively support it.
Added support for Espressif ESP32-C6 and ESP32-C5, enabling Wi-Fi 6 and 5 GHz support on the C5. Closes #92.
Web installer updated to support newer devices. Firmware installation is now easier on devices with native USB (non-UART).
Added support for SPI-based Ethernet devices with configurable control pin.
WiFi and Ethernet can now coexist simultaneously. Wired (Ethernet) is automatically prioritized.
Added support for Gledopto series with Ethernet.
Driving LEDs via USB is now considered stable on native USB devices (non-UART).
USB provisioning via Firefly Luciferin now allows specifying the device name to avoid collisions.
You can now configure the GPIO pin of the built-in LED on your microcontroller.
Luciferin has been added to the Windows Package Manager.
The Windows installation footprint has been significantly reduced.
Enhanced black bar detection with debounce logic to reduce false positives.
Firmware upgraded to Arduino Core 3 (based on IDF5).
Improved LED placement and alignment on the test canvas. Closes #406.
Improved device discovery and UDP communication reliability on complex networks. Closes #400.
Added a reminder for Linux users to add the user to the dialout or uucp group. Closes #371.
GPIO0 was not able to drive the LED strip. Fixed.
Baud rate changes were not applied under load. Fixed.
Improved UI visibility and rendering on the Web Interface.
Fixed incorrect Wi-Fi signal strength with multiple MQTT devices.
Fixed duplicate devices appearing in the Devices tab.
Web Interface showed incorrect framerate when using USB. Fixed.
Arduino Bootstrapper updated (v1.19.7).

description.p: Users running a previous version can use the automatic update feature for both the PC software and the firmware.
